title: Determine the exact value of R(B20, B20)
lean_gate_reason: Do not send this to Lean as a publication-sealing step. PASS 1 established that the exact theorem R(B20,B20)=82 is already in prior art, so formalization would be archival polish on a rediscovery rather than the shortest remaining path to a frontier packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Verification found rediscovery. The Paley witness and conference-graph reduction are real and reproducible, but the exact theorem R(B20,B20)=82 is already settled in prior art, so this does not qualify as a frontier micro-paper target.
family_affinity: Diagonal book Ramsey knife-edge line n = 4m + 1, where B_m avoidance forces conference-graph parameters.
generalization_signal: At the diagonal knife-edge order K_{4m+1}, monochromatic B_m avoidance reduces to srg(4m+1,2m,m-1,m); the reusable part is this reduction, while the endpoint then depends on existence or nonexistence of the corresponding conference graph.
candidate_theorem_slice: The Paley graph on 81 vertices gives an explicit K_81 two-coloring with no monochromatic B20; this is a valid extremal witness, but it supports an already-known exact theorem rather than a new slice.
strongest_honest_claim: The solve packet correctly rederives a classical extremal witness: the Paley graph on 81 vertices has parameters srg(81,40,19,20), so it yields a K_81 coloring with no monochromatic B20. Together with the classical Rousseau-Sheehan prime-power theorem, this recovers the already-known exact value R(B20,B20)=82.
paper_title_hint: The Exact Value of R(B20, B20)
